summary: Strong post‑comeback set with fiery Sugaree → Jack Straw and soaring Stella Blue
blurb: The first set closes tight on a seamless Sugaree → Jack Straw transition, while the second set delivers high‑energy Willie And The Hand Jive into Iko Iko and an emotional Stella Blue. Jerry’s vocals and guitar are freshly invigorated after his return, and the band locks in on Crazy Fingers, Estimated Prophet and Sugar Magnolia.
review:
December 16, 1986 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum found the Dead riding a wave of renewed energy following Jerry Garcia’s comeback from his coma. The night opened with a gritty Hell In A Bucket and moved through familiar terrain before striking gold on the Sugaree → Jack Straw segue – a moment many reviewers called “fiery” and “tight”. The second set kept the momentum rolling: Box of Rain gave way to a punchy Crazy Fingers, which segued into an inspired Estimated Prophet and a soaring Eyes Of The World. A highlight was the drum‑driven burst into Willie And The Hand Jive that erupted into an exuberant Iko Iko, capped by an emotionally charged Stella Blue that had the crowd hanging on every note. The band’s chemistry felt fresh; Jerry’s voice rang clear, Bob stepped forward in the mix delivering standout work on Crazy Fingers and Estimated Prophet, while Brent’s keys provided the usual textural depth. Though a few recordings note tape‑speed quirks and an uneven mix on some SBD sources, the best matrix capture (gd1986-12-16.sbd.miller.77409.flac16) presents a clean, well‑balanced sound that lets these moments shine.

For Deadheads seeking the definitive listening experience, the Miller matrix recording is the clear choice – it balances the energy of the live show with a ~75 % soundboard mix and crisp separation, preserving the nuance of Jerry’s guitar and the high‑octane jam sections. Even listeners limited to audience tapes can still appreciate the raw power of Crazy Fingers and the joyous charge of Sugar Magnolia, but the matrix version offers the most complete and sonically satisfying portrait of a post‑comeback night that truly felt like a fresh start for the band.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Most recordings highlight positive aspects – fresh Jerry vocals, standout Sugaree→Jack Straw segue, high‑energy Willie And The Hand Jive → Iko Iko, and emotional Stella Blue – with no consistent negative criticism of the performance. Mixed sentiments stem mainly from recording quality issues, not musical shortcomings, warranting a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good matrix source (~75 % SBD, 25 % ambient) with clear mix, no dropouts, and captures the standout Sugaree→Jack Straw segue plus high‑energy Iko Iko.

key_highlights: High‑energy Willie And The Hand Jive into Iko Iko
Crazy Fingers and Estimated Prophet showcase tight band chemistry

band_performance.Jerry: Fresh and invigorated after his return; vocals clear on Sugaree, Jack Straw and Stella Blue, guitar work praised throughout especially during the emotional second‑set moments.
band_performance.Bob: Prominent in the mix; delivers strong leads on Crazy Fingers and Estimated Prophet, adding vocal power on Sugar Magnolia.
